edges.description: VCP dysfunction compromises autophagic cargo processing and clearance.
MSP variants reduce nuclear VCP, impairing clearance of intranuclear TDP-43 aggregates and contributing to neurodegeneration.
Persistent proteostasis failure injures muscle, bone, brain, motor neurons, and heart.
Failed autophagic maturation destabilizes lysosomal quality control in differentiated muscle.
Failed lysosomal clearance contributes to the characteristic vacuolar muscle pathology.
Progressive myofiber injury leads to the dominant proximal weakness phenotype.
Degeneration can extend beyond proximal muscle groups to distal limb muscles.
Abnormal bone remodeling produces a Paget disease phenotype in a major subset of patients.
Cerebral degeneration and TDP-43 proteinopathy can produce premature FTD.
Motor neuron vulnerability expands the spectrum into ALS.
Cardiac involvement likely reflects proteostatic and mitochondrial stress in cardiomyocytes.
Progressive muscle and cardiac involvement can culminate in fatal cardiorespiratory decline.
Multisystem muscle and cardiopulmonary involvement can produce exertional dyspnea before or alongside respiratory failure.
Bulbar and skeletal-muscle involvement in the multisystem proteinopathy can produce clinically meaningful dysphagia.
Impaired nuclear VCP function and intranuclear TDP-43 aggregate accumulation in neurons contributes to the FTD phenotype in VCP-MSP.
Motor neurons are particularly vulnerable to nuclear proteostasis failure and TDP-43 pathology, contributing to the ALS phenotype.

nodes.description: Motor neuron disease expands the VCP-MSP spectrum beyond the original IBM/PDB/FTD triad and can occur in the same family as myopathy or FTD.

evidence.reference_title: One family, one gene and three phenotypes: A novel VCP (valosin-containing protein) mutation associated with myopathy with rimmed vacuoles, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis and frontotemporal dementia.
evidence.snippet: Each individual presented with either myopathy with rimmed vacuoles, ALS, or FTD.

nodes.description: Mechanical aids including canes, walkers, orthotics, and wheelchairs are used to support ambulation and independence as limb-girdle and distal muscle weakness progresses in VCP-MSP.

evidence.reference_title: Inclusion Body Myopathy with Paget Disease of Bone and/or Frontotemporal Dementia.
evidence.snippet: mechanical aids (canes, walkers, orthotics, wheelchairs) for ambulation/mobility

nodes.description: Bisphosphonates are indicated to relieve bone pain and reduce disability from Paget disease of bone, which affects approximately half of VCP-MSP patients during the course of their illness.

evidence.reference_title: Inclusion Body Myopathy with Paget Disease of Bone and/or Frontotemporal Dementia.
evidence.snippet: bisphosphonates to relieve pain and disability from PDB

nodes.description: Cardiac involvement is increasingly recognized in VCP-MSP and may include dilated or hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with conduction or diastolic abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiomyopathy in valosin-containing protein multisystem proteinopathy: Evaluation, diagnosis, and management.
evidence.snippet: Emerging evidence from animal models and human case studies suggests that VCP dysfunction disrupts cardiomyocyte homeostasis, impairs protein degradation, and alters mitochondrial function, leading to maladaptive cardiac remodeling and susceptibility to dilated or hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

nodes.description: VCP is required for normal maturation of ubiquitin-containing autophagosomes. Disease-associated VCP dysfunction leads to accumulation of autophagic structures and persistence of ubiquitinated cargo, linking the proximal proteostasis defect to muscle and neuronal pathology.

evidence.reference_title: Inclusion body myopathy, Paget's disease of the bone and fronto-temporal dementia: a disorder of autophagy.
evidence.snippet: In addition, IBMPFD mutations in p97/VCP lead to accumulation of autophagic structures in patient and transgenic animal tissue. This is likely due to a defect in p97/VCP-mediated autophagosome maturation.

evidence.reference_title: VCP/p97 is essential for maturation of ubiquitin-containing autophagosomes and this function is impaired by mutations that cause IBMPFD.
evidence.snippet: We conclude that VCP is essential for maturation of ubiquitin-containing autophagosomes and that defect in this function may contribute to IBMPFD pathogenesis.

nodes.description: Distal limb muscles can also become weak and wasted, contributing to gait impairment and disability.

evidence.reference_title: Case report of a family with hereditary inclusion body myopathy with VCP gene variant and literature review.
evidence.snippet: Myopathy is the most frequent manifestation characterized by slowly progressing weakness of proximal and distal limb muscles.

nodes.description: Bulbar dysfunction can produce choking episodes and swallowing difficulty in a clinically meaningful subset of patients.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic diversity in an international Cure VCP Disease registry.
evidence.snippet: Thirteen patients (22%) reported dysphagia and 25 patients (42%) reported dyspnea on exertion.

nodes.description: Exertional dyspnea is a common patient-reported manifestation that likely reflects combined respiratory muscle weakness and cardiopulmonary burden.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic diversity in an international Cure VCP Disease registry.
evidence.snippet: Thirteen patients (22%) reported dysphagia and 25 patients (42%) reported dyspnea on exertion.

nodes.description: Premature behavioral and personality-predominant frontotemporal dementia is part of the classic VCP disease triad.

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: Findings of premature FTD affecting behavior and personality are seen in a third of affected individuals.

nodes.description: Management of VCP-associated cardiomyopathy currently extrapolates from standard heart-failure practice using neurohormonal blockade.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiomyopathy in valosin-containing protein multisystem proteinopathy: Evaluation, diagnosis, and management.
evidence.snippet: Current treatment strategies are extrapolated from heart failure guidelines, incorporating neurohormonal blockades with angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors, beta-blockers, and mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists.

nodes.description: Most molecularly diagnosed cases are caused by heterozygous missense variants affecting the VCP coding sequence.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype study in patients with valosin-containing protein mutations associated with multisystem proteinopathy.
evidence.snippet: Most cases are caused by heterozygous missense mutations in valosin-containing protein (VCP) (1), (2).

nodes.description: VCP is required not only for cytoplasmic autophagy/lysosomal clearance but also for nuclear protein homeostasis. MSP-associated VCP variants reduce nuclear VCP levels, impairing clearance of insoluble intranuclear TDP-43 aggregates. This nuclear proteostasis defect is an emerging mechanistic link between VCP dysfunction and the neurodegeneration seen in FTD and ALS within VCP-MSP, and pharmacologic VCP activation restores aggregate clearance in cell-based models.

evidence.reference_title: VCP activator reverses nuclear proteostasis defects and enhances TDP-43 aggregate clearance in multisystem proteinopathy models.
evidence.snippet: Our findings suggest that VCP function is important for nuclear protein homeostasis, that impaired nuclear proteostasis may contribute to MSP, and that VCP activation may be a potential therapeutic by virtue of enhancing the clearance of intranuclear protein aggregates.

nodes.description: Progressive shoulder- and pelvic-girdle weakness is the dominant and most frequent clinical manifestation of VCP-MSP.

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: Approximately 90% of the affected persons in the study have myopathy or muscle weakness particularly of the shoulder and hip girdles, which can lead to loss of walking ability and even death by complications of respiratory and cardiac failure.

nodes.description: In differentiated skeletal muscle, VCP dysfunction causes damaged lysosomes, persistent lysosomal stress signaling, vacuolar pathology, and myofiber injury. This muscle-specific downstream state helps explain the prominent vacuolar myopathy and weakness of VCP-MSP.

evidence.reference_title: VCP maintains lysosomal homeostasis and TFEB activity in differentiated skeletal muscle.
evidence.snippet: Surprisingly, knockout muscle demonstrated a necrotic myopathy with increased macroautophagic/autophagic proteins and damaged lysosomes.

evidence.reference_title: Valosin containing protein associated inclusion body myopathy: abnormal vacuolization, autophagy and cell fusion in myoblasts.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, mutant myoblasts show increased autophagy when cultured in the absence of nutrients, as well as defective cell fusion and increased apoptosis.

nodes.description: VCP-MSP is a multisystem proteinopathy in which abnormal protein clearance produces degenerative pathology across skeletal muscle, bone, cerebrum, motor neurons, and sometimes heart. Ubiquitin- and TDP-43-positive inclusions are recurrent pathological hallmarks across affected tissues, and recent work further implicates defective nuclear proteostasis.

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: Inclusions seen in the muscle, brain and heart in VCP disease contain ubiquitin, beta amyloid and TDP-43, also seen in other neurodegenerative disorders thus implicating common pathways in their pathogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: The homozygote VCP(R155H/R155H) mouse model exhibits accelerated human VCP-associated disease pathology.
evidence.snippet: The VCP(R155H/R155H) mice manifest prominent muscle, heart, brain and spinal cord pathology, including striking mitochondrial abnormalities, in addition to disrupted autophagy and ubiquitin pathologies.

evidence.reference_title: VCP activator reverses nuclear proteostasis defects and enhances TDP-43 aggregate clearance in multisystem proteinopathy models.
evidence.snippet: We found that these diseases exhibit a common pathologic feature: ubiquitinated intranuclear inclusions affecting myocytes, osteoclasts, and neurons.

nodes.description: Ventilatory support is used in patients with respiratory involvement, especially when orthopnea or nocturnal insufficiency develops.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic diversity in an international Cure VCP Disease registry.
evidence.snippet: Twenty-five patients (42%) noticed their shortness of breath when they were walking, eating, bathing, or dressing. Twelve patients (21%) had orthopnea, seven patients (12%) used some machine which support ventilation or supplementary oxygen, and two patients (3%) were not able to sleep enough despite using extra pillows and ventilatory support machines.

nodes.description: Accelerated and abnormal bone remodeling produces bone pain, enlargement, and fracture susceptibility in a substantial subset of patients.

evidence.reference_title: VCP disease associated with myopathy, Paget disease of bone and frontotemporal dementia: review of a unique disorder.
evidence.snippet: About half of affected study participants have Paget disease of bone characterized by abnormal rates of bone growth that can result in bone pain, enlargement and fractures.

nodes.description: Physical therapy is used as supportive rehabilitation to preserve mobility, function, and participation in progressive VCP myopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic diversity in an international Cure VCP Disease registry.
evidence.snippet: The Cure VCP Disease Patient Registry provides the necessary tools for remote recruitment and enrollment. Sharing information between patients and physician/researchers through the registry will accelerate the establishment of appropriate clinical outcome measurement and/or biomarkers, and implementation of physical therapy and disease modifying therapies in future.

nodes.description: Recurrent pathogenic alleles include hotspot Arg155 substitutions and multiple Arg159 substitutions represented across published cohorts.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype study in patients with valosin-containing protein mutations associated with multisystem proteinopathy.
evidence.snippet: The mutations associated with IBMPFD and/or familial ALS are all exonic missense mutations. In particular, the R155 locus is a mutation hotspot.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlations in valosin-containing protein disease: a retrospective muticentre study.
evidence.snippet: c.464G>A (p.Arg155His) was the most frequent variant, identified in the 28%.

nodes.description: Progressive neuromuscular and cardiac disease can culminate in respiratory failure, an important cause of late mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Utilization of CoRDS registry to monitor quality of life in patients with VCP multisystem proteinopathy.
evidence.snippet: Due to its progressive nature, death normally occurs in their sixties due to respiratory and cardiac failure.

nodes.description: Rimmed vacuoles are a characteristic muscle-biopsy finding reflecting the underlying vacuolar proteinopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Provisional practice recommendation for the management of myopathy in VCP-associated multisystem proteinopathy.
evidence.snippet: Muscle biopsy is important in cases of diagnostic uncertainty or lack of a definitive pathogenic genetic variant since rimmed vacuoles (present in ~40% cases) are considered a hallmark of VCP myopathy.

nodes.description: Speech and language therapy addresses bulbar dysfunction including dysphagia, occasional choking, and speech disturbances affecting a clinically meaningful subset of VCP-MSP patients.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic diversity in an international Cure VCP Disease registry.
evidence.snippet: Thirteen patients (22%) had experienced occasional choking and one patient needed a modified diet. Seven patients (12%) were also aware of their excessive saliva. Six patients (10%) had detectable speech disturbances and could be intelligible with repeating.

nodes.description: Pathogenic VCP variants disrupt a multifunctional AAA+ ATPase that normally coordinates ubiquitin-dependent protein turnover, ER-associated degradation, autophagy, mitophagy, and lysophagy. The shared proximal consequence is impaired cellular proteostasis across tissues that are selectively vulnerable in VCP-MSP.

evidence.reference_title: Valosin-Containing Protein (VCP): A Review of Its Diverse Molecular Functions and Clinical Phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: VCP is crucial to a multitude of cellular functions including protein quality control, endoplasmic reticulum-associated degradation (ERAD), autophagy, mitophagy, lysophagy, stress granule formation and clearance, DNA replication and mitosis, DNA damage response including nucleotide excision repair, ATM- and ATR-mediated damage response, homologous repair and non-homologous end joining.

evidence.reference_title: VCP-related myopathy: a case series and a review of literature.
evidence.snippet: The valosin-containing protein (VCP), a widely expressed protein, controls the ubiquitin-proteasome system, endolysosomal sorting, and autophagy to maintain cellular proteostasis.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype study in patients with valosin-containing protein mutations associated with multisystem proteinopathy.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in valosin-containing protein (VCP), an ATPase involved in protein degradation and autophagy, cause VCP disease, a progressive autosomal dominant adult onset multisystem proteinopathy.
